CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2016 Continued To Jul-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHON M. SPINELLI, ESQ. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel No. 650-364-8200 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter was heard at 2:00 p.m. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to the Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction, Plaintiffs' request for a preliminary injunction is GRANTED. No opposition has been filed. The court finds that Plaintiffs have shown a likelihood of success and the balance of hardships tips in favor of provisional relief at this time. The injunction is substantially identical to the temporary restraining order in place. Plaintiffs shall prepare and submit a proposed order setting forth the specific terms of the injunction and e-mail the proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Todd Roberts of Ropers Majeski telephone number 650-364-8200 for Plaintiff.
Edward Susolik of Callahan & Blaine, APLC 714-241-4444 for Defendant Phillipe Nicolas.

Mr. Roberts presented a stipulation to continue the matter. The Court vacated its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction is continued to February 19, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 per agreement of the parties. Order continuing the matter signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
